response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and systems engineer whose career is built almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no demonstrated capital markets or fixed income domain knowledge. His C++ exposure is limited to a short stint at National Instruments (~6 months) and a university thesis project, far short of the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development required. There is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle experience, no familiarity with financial instruments or trading systems, and no mention of the Summit platform or messaging frameworks like Kafka. He does not meet the mandatory technical or domain requirements for this role.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals including TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development practiced across multiple roles
Demonstrated communication and knowledge-sharing skills through conference talks, workshops, and training courses
Java development experience listed, which is a desirable secondary skill for the role
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI plus a thesis project) versus the 5–10 years required, and no evidence of Windows platform C++ work
Zero capital markets, investment banking, or fixed income domain exposure across the entire employment history
No mention of SQL Server or Oracle; SQL is listed as a generic skill with no enterprise RDBMS depth demonstrated

key_factors.factor: Capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
